message:
fix(scanf): %n must not increment scanf return count + harness polish

Three related polish items on top of 54df9ea1:

scanf engine
- ScanValue::CharsConsumed (the %n result) was being counted toward
  ScanResult.count alongside the regular conversions, but POSIX/glibc
  exclude %n from the assignment count: "%d%n" against "42" returns 1,
  not 2. Tests expecting count=2 were locking in the wrong contract.
  Engine now distinguishes CharsConsumed and skips the count++ for it
  while still pushing the value into `values` so the ABI layer can
  store it through the caller's pointer. Updated the unit test to pin
  count=1 for the %d%n case.

scanf conformance test harness
- Drop the dead `KNOWN_EXECUTION_GAPS` / `case_is_known_execution_gap`
  / `case_needs_multi_value_support` / `case_needs_multi_spec_support`
  / `count_scanf_specs` helpers and their early-continue guards in
  `scanf_conformance_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case` and
  `scanf_conformance_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix`.
  After 54df9ea1 emptied the gap list and the harness gained
  multi-arg/multi-value coverage, every case is now exercisable, so
  the filtering scaffolding is unreachable. Skipped counter stays for
  the post-loop assertion but is fixed at 0.

scanf conformance lib
- Add SAFETY: comments explaining the variadic dispatch contract for
  the impl-side and host-side branches of `call_sscanf_multi` (the
  arity-fanout match introduced in 54df9ea1). Documents that input/
  format are live NUL-terminated C strings owned by the fixture
  executor and that each scratch slot is a distinct, aligned, large
  enough destination for the supported scanf types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
